547	/-- The geometric Čech backbone, lifted to a **simplicial object in `Over X`**: each
548	fibre power carries its structure map to `X` (the augmentation of `coverCechNerve`),
549	and the face/degeneracy maps are `X`-morphisms by naturality of the augmentation into
550	the constant functor. Project-local; obtained from `coverCechNerve` by
551	`CategoryTheory.Over.lift` applied to the augmentation natural transformation. -/
552	noncomputable def coverCechNerveOver (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) : SimplicialObject (Over X) :=
553	  Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom
554	
555	/-- The over-category Čech backbone as an **augmented** simplicial object in `Over X`:
556	`coverCechNerveOver` augmented by the terminal object `Over.mk (𝟙 X)` of `Over X`. The
557	augmentation map at each simplicial level is the unique morphism to the terminal object
558	(its underlying scheme map is the level's structure map to `X`), and the augmentation
559	coherence condition holds automatically because the augmentation target is terminal.
560	Project-local. -/
561	noncomputable def coverCechNerveOverAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
562	    SimplicialObject.Augmented (Over X) :=
563	  SimplicialObject.augment (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰) (Over.mk (𝟙 X))
564	    (Over.mkIdTerminal.from _)
565	    (fun _ _ _ => Over.mkIdTerminal.hom_ext _ _)

567	/-- The cosimplicial `O_X`-module obtained by post-composing the over-category Čech
568	backbone `coverCechNerveOver` (read contravariantly, via `Functor.leftOp`) with the
569	push–pull functor `pushPullFunctor F = G`. This is the underlying cosimplicial object
570	of `CechNerve` (before adjoining the augmentation `F`). Project-local. -/
571	noncomputable def cechNerveCosimplicial (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
572	    CosimplicialObject X.Modules :=
573	  (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰 : SimplexCategoryᵒᵖ ⥤ Over X).rightOp ⋙ pushPullFunctor F
574	
575	/-- **Čech nerve of an affine open cover** (Stacks, Cohomology of Schemes, §Čech
576	cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves).
577	
578	For a scheme `X`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰 : X = ⋃ Uᵢ` and a quasi-coherent
579	sheaf `F`, the *Čech nerve* is the augmented cosimplicial object of `O_X`-modules whose
580	object in simplicial degree `p` is the product, over the `(p+1)`-tuples `(i₀,…,i_p)` of
581	indices, of the direct images `(j_{i₀…i_p})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})` of the restriction of
582	`F` to the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{i₀…i_p} = U_{i₀} ∩ ⋯ ∩ U_{i_p}` along the open
583	immersion `j_{i₀…i_p} : U_{i₀…i_p} ↪ X`. Faces are the restriction maps that omit one
584	index, degeneracies repeat one index, and the augmentation in degree `-1` is `F` itself
585	on all of `X`. When `X` is separated each intersection `U_{i₀…i_p}` is affine.
586	
587	This is now *constructed*, not postulated: the geometric backbone `coverCechNerveOverAug`
588	(the augmented Čech nerve of the cover as an augmented simplicial object in `Over X`,
589	unconditional because `Scheme` has all finite limits) is read contravariantly
590	(`SimplicialObject.Augmented.rightOp`) and whiskered by the push–pull functor
591	`pushPullFunctor F = G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. The whiskering
592	transports both the cosimplicial structure and the augmentation, so the augmentation
593	point is `G` applied to the terminal `Over X`-object `⟨X, 𝟙 X⟩`, namely
594	`(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F ≅ F`. The functor laws of `G` (`pushPullMap_id`, `pushPullMap_comp`)
595	are what make this assembly legitimate.
596	
597	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
598	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
599	noncomputable def CechNerve (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
600	    CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules :=
601	  (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.whiskeringObj (Over X)ᵒᵖ X.Modules (pushPullFunctor F)).obj
602	    (coverCechNerveOverAug 𝒰).rightOp
603	
604	/-- **Relative {\v C}ech complex from a cosimplicial nerve** (coherence-free
605	plumbing). Given `f : X ⟶ S` and an augmented cosimplicial object `N` of
606	`O_X`-modules, produce the relative {\v C}ech cochain complex in `QCoh(S)` by:
607	forgetting the augmentation (`CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop`), pushing the
608	cosimplicial object forward along `f` (`CosimplicialObject.whiskering` applied to
609	`Scheme.Modules.pushforward f`), and taking the alternating coface-map cochain
610	complex (`alternatingCofaceMapComplex`). This is the entire passage `CechNerve ↦
611	CechComplex`, and it uses no `pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` coherence — only
612	the (pre)additivity of `S.Modules`. Project-local. -/
613	noncomputable def relativeCechComplexOfNerve (f : X ⟶ S)
614	    (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented X.Modules) : CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
615	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex S.Modules).obj
616	    (((CosimplicialObject.whiskering X.Modules S.Modules).obj
617	        (Scheme.Modules.pushforward f)).obj (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N))
618	
619	/-- **Relative Čech complex of a quasi-coherent sheaf** (Stacks, Cohomology of
620	Schemes, `lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`).
621	
622	For `f : X ⟶ S`, a finite affine open cover `𝒰` of `X` (with all intersections
623	affine, e.g. `X` separated) and a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`, the *relative Čech
624	complex* `Č•(𝒰, F)` is the cochain complex of `O_S`-modules with degree-`p` term
625	```
626	  Čᵖ(𝒰, F) = ∏_{(i₀,…,i_p)} (f|_{U_{i₀…i_p}})_* (F|_{U_{i₀…i_p}}),
627	```
628	and differential the alternating sum of the restriction maps
629	`(d s)_{i₀…i_{p+1}} = Σⱼ (-1)ʲ s_{i₀…î_j…i_{p+1}}|_{U_{i₀…i_{p+1}}}`. Over an
630	affine `U = Spec A` with `F|_U = M~` and a standard cover by the `D(fᵢ)`, this is
631	the complex of localisations `∏ M_{f_{i₀}} → ∏ M_{f_{i₀}f_{i₁}} → ⋯`. Each term
632	is quasi-coherent because the intersections are affine and the pushforward of a
633	quasi-coherent sheaf along a quasi-compact quasi-separated morphism is
634	quasi-coherent.
635	
636	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
637	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`. -/
638	noncomputable def CechComplex (f : X ⟶ S) (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
639	    CochainComplex S.Modules ℕ :=
640	  -- Construction (Stacks): apply the relative pushforward `f_*` over each finite
641	  -- intersection to the Čech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F`, then take the alternating-sum
642	  -- Čech differential. This is exactly the coherence-free plumbing
643	  -- `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`, so `CechComplex` is genuinely *defined* in terms
644	  -- of the nerve: an axiom-clean `CechNerve` immediately yields an axiom-clean
645	  -- `CechComplex`. The only remaining hole is `CechNerve` itself.
646	  relativeCechComplexOfNerve f (CechNerve 𝒰 F)

648	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — the augmented Čech complex on `X`
649	
650	The lemma `cechAugmented_exact` (`lem:cech_augmented_resolution`) asserts that the
651	augmented {\v C}ech complex `0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` of a quasi-coherent sheaf `F`
652	over an affine open cover `𝒰` is **exact** in `QCoh(X)` — i.e. the {\v C}ech nerve
653	is a resolution of `F`. This is the *un-pushed* (over `X`, not relative to `f`)
654	counterpart of `CechComplex`/`relativeCechComplexOfNerve`. We record here the
655	structural ingredients that the exactness statement is phrased against: the
656	un-augmented {\v C}ech complex on `X` (`cechComplexOnX`), obtained from the {\v C}ech
657	nerve exactly as `relativeCechComplexOfNerve` does but with the *identity*
658	pushforward — i.e. directly, with no `f_*`. -/
659	
660	/-- **The (un-augmented) {\v C}ech cochain complex of `F` on `X`.** This is the
661	alternating-coface-map complex `C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` of the underlying cosimplicial object
662	of the {\v C}ech nerve `CechNerve 𝒰 F` (its augmentation `F` is dropped). It is the
663	`f = 𝟙`/un-pushed analogue of `relativeCechComplexOfNerve`: the same coherence-free
664	plumbing without the relative pushforward `f_*`, so it is the complex whose
665	exactness (after re-adjoining the augmentation `F → C⁰`) is the content of
666	`cechAugmented_exact`. Project-local. -/
667	noncomputable def cechComplexOnX (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
668	    CochainComplex X.Modules ℕ :=
669	  (AlgebraicTopology.alternatingCofaceMapComplex X.Modules).obj
670	    (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F))
671	
672	/-- **The augmentation point of the {\v C}ech nerve is `F`.** The augmentation
673	object `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).left` of the {\v C}ech nerve is `(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F` (the
674	push–pull functor applied to the terminal `Over X`-object `⟨X, 𝟙 X⟩`), canonically
675	isomorphic to `F` via the unitors `pushforwardId`/`pullbackId`. Project-local. -/
676	noncomputable def cechNervePointIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
677	    (CechNerve 𝒰 F).left ≅ F :=
678	  (Scheme.Modules.pushforwardId X).app ((Scheme.Modules.pullback (𝟙 X)).obj F) ≪≫
679	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackId X).app F
680	
681	/-- **The augmentation map `ε : F → C⁰` of the {\v C}ech nerve.** Built from the
682	augmentation natural transformation of the augmented cosimplicial object
683	`CechNerve 𝒰 F` (the map `(CechNerve 𝒰 F).left ⟶ C⁰`), pre-composed with the unitor
684	`cechNervePointIso` identifying the augmentation point with `F`. This is the map
685	prepended to `cechComplexOnX` to form the augmented {\v C}ech complex. Project-local. -/
686	noncomputable def cechAugmentation (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
687	    F ⟶ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).X 0 :=
688	  (cechNervePointIso 𝒰 F).inv ≫ (CechNerve 𝒰 F).hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0)
689	
690	/-- **Augmentation kills the first alternating-coface differential** (abstract form).
691	For any augmented cosimplicial object `N` in a preadditive category, the augmentation
692	map `N.hom.app [0] : N.left ⟶ (drop N).obj [0]` followed by the degree-0 alternating
693	coface differential `objD (drop N) 0 = δ⁰ - δ¹` is zero. This is the cosimplicial
694	augmentation identity (`ε ≫ δ⁰ = ε ≫ δ¹`, from naturality of `N.hom` against the two
695	coface maps `[0] ⟶ [1]`); stated abstractly so the proof does not unfold any concrete
696	(heavily whiskered) nerve. Project-local. -/
697	private lemma augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero
698	    {C : Type*} [Category C] [Preadditive C] (N : CosimplicialObject.Augmented C) :
699	    N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) ≫
700	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
701	        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj N) 0 = 0 := by
702	  -- Ascribe the augmentation's codomain to the `𝟭`-free form `N.right.obj ⦋0⦌`; this is
703	  -- the `Comma`'s right functor being `𝟭`, which otherwise pins the composition's middle
704	  -- object to `(𝟭).obj N.right` and blocks every additive distribution lemma's instance.
705	  have hnat : ∀ i : Fin 2,
706	      (N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) : N.left ⟶ N.right.obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) ≫
707	        N.right.δ i = N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 1) := by
708	    intro i
709	    have h := N.hom.naturality (SimplexCategory.δ i)
710	    simpa using h.symm
711	  show (N.hom.app (SimplexCategory.mk 0) : N.left ⟶ N.right.obj (SimplexCategory.mk 0)) ≫
712	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD N.right 0 = 0
713	  simp only [AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD]
714	  rw [Fin.sum_univ_two]
715	  simp only [Fin.val_zero, Fin.val_one, pow_zero, pow_one, one_zsmul, neg_one_zsmul]
716	  -- `erw` (defeq-matching) is needed: the `Comma`'s right functor `𝟭` pins the
717	  -- composition's middle object to `(𝟭).obj N.right`, so `comp_add`/`comp_neg`'s
718	  -- instances only match up to reducible defeq, not syntactically.
719	  erw [Preadditive.comp_add, Preadditive.comp_neg, hnat 0, hnat 1, add_neg_cancel]
720	
721	/-- **The augmentation composes to zero with `d⁰`.** The {\v C}ech augmentation
722	`ε : F → C⁰` followed by the first {\v C}ech differential `d⁰ : C⁰ → C¹` is zero,
723	the cochain-complex condition needed to prepend `ε` to `cechComplexOnX`. It is the
724	cosimplicial augmentation identity: `ε ≫ δ⁰ = ε ≫ δ¹` (naturality of the
725	augmentation natural transformation against the two coface maps `[0] ⟶ [1]`), so the
726	alternating sum `ε ≫ (δ⁰ - δ¹)` vanishes. Project-local. -/
727	lemma cechAugmentation_comp_d (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
728	    cechAugmentation 𝒰 F ≫ (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).d 0 1 = 0 := by
729	  rw [cechAugmentation, Category.assoc]
730	  have hd : (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).d 0 1 =
731	      AlgebraicTopology.AlternatingCofaceMapComplex.objD
732	        (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.drop.obj (CechNerve 𝒰 F)) 0 := rfl
733	  rw [hd]
734	  erw [augmentation_comp_alternatingCofaceMap_objD_zero (CechNerve 𝒰 F)]
735	  exact Limits.comp_zero
736	
737	/-- **The augmented {\v C}ech complex on `X`** (Stacks
738	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent-trivial`). The cochain complex
739	`0 → F → C⁰ → C¹ → ⋯` obtained from the un-augmented {\v C}ech complex `cechComplexOnX`
740	by prepending the augmentation `ε : F → C⁰` (`cechAugmentation`) in degree `0` — so
741	`(cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).X 0 = F` and `(cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F).X (p+1) = Cᵖ`. The
742	augmentation condition `ε ≫ d⁰ = 0` is `cechAugmentation_comp_d`. The lemma
743	`cech_augmented_resolution` (`cechAugmented_exact`) asserts this complex is exact —
744	i.e. the {\v C}ech nerve is a resolution of `F` in `QCoh(X)`. Project-local. -/
745	noncomputable def cechAugmentedComplex (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) :
746	    CochainComplex X.Modules ℕ :=
747	  (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F).augment (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F) (cechAugmentation_comp_d 𝒰 F)
748	
749	/-- **The Čech complex computes the higher direct images** (Stacks 02KE;
750	`lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent` and
751	`lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application`).
752	
753	Let `f : X ⟶ S` be separated and quasi-compact, `F` a quasi-coherent
754	`O_X`-module, and `𝒰` a finite affine open cover of `X` (so, by separatedness,
755	every intersection is affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative Čech
756	complex compute the higher direct images: for every `i ≥ 0` there is a canonical
757	isomorphism of `O_S`-modules
758	```
759	  Hⁱ(Č•(𝒰, F)) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F.
760	```
761	In particular, over an affine base `S = Spec A`, taking global sections gives
762	`Hⁱ(X, F) = Čⁱ(𝒰, F) = H⁰(S, Rⁱ f_* F)` as `A`-modules.
763	
764	We state the isomorphism as `Nonempty (… ≅ …)` and compare against the
765	derived-functor higher direct image `higherDirectImage` wherever the latter is
766	defined (`HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules`).
767	
768	Route A: reduce to `S` affine; the augmented Čech complex is a termwise
769	`f_*`-acyclic resolution of `F` (each term is a product of pushforwards over
770	affine intersections, acyclic by `CechAcyclic.affine` via the P3b bridge), so
771	the P4 acyclic-resolution lemma (`rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`) gives
772	`Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F`. -/
773	theorem cech_computes_higherDirectImage [HasInjectiveResolutions X.Modules]
774	    (f : X ⟶ S) [QuasiCompact f] [IsSeparated f] (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
775	    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) (i : ℕ) :
776	    Nonempty ((CechComplex f 𝒰 F).homology i ≅ higherDirectImage f i F) := by
777	  -- Route A: reduce to `S` affine; the Čech complex is a termwise acyclic
778	  -- resolution of `F` (by `CechAcyclic.affine`), so `rightDerivedIsoOfAcyclicResolution`
779	  -- gives `Hⁱ(f_* C•) ≅ Rⁱ f_* F`.
780	  sorry

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) (h : Z ⟶ T)

/-- The pushforward functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pushforward : X.Modules ⥤ Y.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforward f.toRingCatSheafHom

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_obj (M : X.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    Γ((pushforward f).obj M, U) = Γ(M, f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_obj_presheaf_map {U V : Y.Opens} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    ((pushforward f).obj M).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map ((Opens.map f.base).map i).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma pushforward_map_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforward f).map φ).app U = φ.app (f ⁻¹ᵁ U) := rfl

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes. -/
def pullback : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom

/-- The pullback functor for categories of sheaves of modules over schemes
is left adjoint to the pushforward functor. -/
def pullbackPushforwardAdjunction : pullback f ⊣ pushforward f :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction _

section

attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryCoproducts
  preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBinaryProducts

instance : (pullback f).IsLeftAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).IsRightAdjoint := (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f).isRightAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _
instance : (pullback f).Additive := Functor.additive_of_preservesBinaryBiproducts _

end

variable (X) in
/-- The pushforward of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pushforwardId : pushforward (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_hom_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma pushforwardId_inv_app_app : ((pushforwardId X).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl

variable (X) in
/-- The pullback of sheaves of modules by the identity morphism identifies
to the identity functor. -/
def pullbackId : pullback (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackId _

variable (X) in
lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom :
    conjugateEquiv .id (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (𝟙 X)) (pullbackId X).hom =
      (pushforwardId X).inv :=
  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom _

/-- The composition of two pushforward functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
identify to the pushforward for the composition. -/
def pushforwardComp :
    pushforward f ⋙ pushforward g ≅ pushforward (f ≫ g) :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _

@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_hom_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).hom.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
@[simp] lemma pushforwardComp_inv_app_app (U) : ((pushforwardComp f g).inv.app M).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl

/-- The composition of two pullback functors for sheaves of modules on schemes
identify to the pullback for the composition. -/
def pullbackComp :
    pullback g ⋙ pullback f ≅ pullback (f ≫ g) :=
  SheafOfModules.pullbackComp _ _

/-- Pushforwards along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def pushforwardCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pushforward f ≅ pushforward g :=
    pushforwardNatIso _ (Opens.mapIso _ _ (hf ▸ rfl)) ≪≫
      SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by cat_disch)

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforwardCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl

@[simp] lemma pushforwardCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((pushforwardCongr hf).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (hf ▸ rfl)).op := rfl

/-- Inverse images along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def pullbackCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) : pullback f ≅ pullback g :=
  eqToIso (hf ▸ rfl)

lemma conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv :
    conjugateEquiv ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).comp (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f))
      (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (f ≫ g)) (pullbackComp f g).inv =
    (pushforwardComp f g).hom :=
  SheafOfModules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv _ _

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc]
lemma pseudofunctor_associativity :
    (pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)).inv ≫
      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackComp g h).inv _ ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫
        Functor.whiskerLeft _ (pullbackComp f g).hom ≫ (pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h).hom =
    eqToHom (by simp) := by
  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (g ≫ h)
  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackComp g h) (pullback f)
  let e₃ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback h) (pullbackComp f g)
  let e₄ := pullbackComp (f ≫ g) h
  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.inv ≫ (Functor.associator _ _ _).hom ≫ e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = _
  have : e₃.hom ≫ e₄.hom = (Functor.associator _ _ _).inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₁.hom :=
    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_assoc.{u}
      h.toRingCatSheafHom g.toRingCatSheafHom f.toRingCatSheafHom)
  simp [this]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc]
lemma pseudofunctor_left_unitality :
    (pullbackComp f (𝟙 Y)).inv ≫
      Functor.whiskerRight (pullbackId Y).hom (pullback f) ≫ (Functor.leftUnitor _).hom =
        eqToHom (by simp) := by
  let e₁ := pullbackComp f (𝟙 _)
  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerRight (pullbackId Y) (pullback f)
  let e₃ := (pullback f).leftUnitor
  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_id_comp.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
  simp [← this]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
@[reassoc]
lemma pseudofunctor_right_unitality :
    (pullbackComp (𝟙 X) f).inv ≫
      Functor.whiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId X).hom ≫ (Functor.rightUnitor _).hom =
        eqToHom (by simp) := by
  let e₁ := pullbackComp (𝟙 _) f
  let e₂ := Functor.isoWhiskerLeft (pullback f) (pullbackId _)
  let e₃ := (pullback f).rightUnitor
  change e₁.inv ≫ e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom = _
  have : e₁.hom = e₂.hom ≫ e₃.hom :=
    congr_arg Iso.hom (SheafOfModules.pullback_comp_id.{u} f.toRingCatSheafHom)
  simp [← this]

set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
attribute [local simp] pseudofunctor_associativity pseudofunctor_left_unitality
  pseudofunctor_right_unitality Bicategory.toNatTrans_conjugateEquiv
  conjugateEquiv_pullbackId_hom Adjunction.ofCat_comp conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv in
/-- The pseudofunctor from `Schemeᵒᵖ` to the bicategory of adjunctions which sends
a scheme `X` to the category `X.Modules` of sheaves of modules over `X`.
(This contains both the covariant and the contravariant functorialities of
these categories.) -/
@[simps! obj_obj map_l map_r map_adj
  mapId_hom_τl mapId_hom_τr mapId_inv_τl mapId_inv_τr
  mapComp_hom_τl mapComp_hom_τr mapComp_inv_τl mapComp_inv_τr]
def pseudofunctor :
    Pseudofunctor (LocallyDiscrete Scheme.{u}ᵒᵖ) (Adj Cat) :=
  LocallyDiscrete.mkPseudofunctor
    (fun X ↦ Adj.mk (Cat.of X.unop.Modules))
    (fun f ↦ .mk (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f.unop).toCat)
    (fun _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackId _))
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardId _).symm))
    (fun _ _ ↦ Adj.iso₂Mk (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pullbackComp _ _).symm)
        (Cat.Hom.isoMk (pushforwardComp _ _)))

end Functorial

noncomputable section Restriction

variable (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g]

/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
but has better defeqs. -/
def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }
  SheafOfModules.pushforward (F := f.opensFunctor)
    ⟨Functor.whiskerRight α (forget₂ CommRingCat RingCat)⟩

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
abbrev restrict (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] : X.Modules :=
  (restrictFunctor f).obj M

@[simp] lemma restrict_obj (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] (U) :
    Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U) := rfl

@[simp] lemma restrict_map (M : Y.Modules) (f : X ⟶ Y) [IsOpenImmersion f] {U V} (i : U ⟶ V) :
    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl

/-- The restriction of a module along an open immersion. -/
def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
  · ext U x
    have : (f.appIso U.unop).inv ≫ f.app _ ≫
      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by
      rw [Scheme.Hom.appIso_inv_app_assoc, ← Functor.map_comp, ← X.presheaf.map_id]; rfl
    exact congr($this x)

instance : IsIso (restrictAdjunction f).counit :=
  inferInstanceAs (IsIso <| (restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso f).hom)

instance : (restrictFunctor f).IsLeftAdjoint := (restrictAdjunction f).isLeftAdjoint
instance : (pushforward f).Full := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.full
instance : (pushforward f).Faithful := (restrictAdjunction f).fullyFaithfulROfIsIsoCounit.faithful

@[simp]
lemma restrictAdjunction_unit_app_app (M : Y.Modules) (U : Y.Opens) :
    ((restrictAdjunction f).unit.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (homOfLE (f.image_preimage_le U)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictAdjunction_counit_app_app (M : X.Modules) (U : X.Opens) :
    ((restrictAdjunction f).counit.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm).op := rfl

/-- Restriction is naturally isomorphic to the inverse image. -/
def restrictFunctorIsoPullback : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f :=
  (restrictAdjunction f).leftAdjointUniq (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction f)

/-- Restriction along the identity is isomorphic to the identity. -/
def restrictFunctorId : restrictFunctor (𝟙 X) ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents (fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp))) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr
      (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorId_hom_app_app :
    (restrictFunctorId.hom.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show U = 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U by simp)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorId_inv_app_app :
    (restrictFunctorId.inv.app M).app U =
      M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (show 𝟙 X ''ᵁ U = U by simp)).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along the composition is isomorphic to the composition of restrictions. -/
def restrictFunctorComp : restrictFunctor (f ≫ g) ≅ restrictFunctor g ⋙ restrictFunctor f :=
  have : (f.opensFunctor ⋙ g.opensFunctor).IsContinuous
      (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Z) :=
    Functor.isContinuous_comp _ _ _ (Opens.grothendieckTopology _) _
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp)) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; simp [← Functor.map_comp, SheafedSpace.sheaf]) ≪≫
    (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _).symm

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorComp_hom_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorComp_inv_app_app (M : Z.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorComp f g).inv.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp)).op := rfl

/-- Restriction along equal morphisms are isomorphic. -/
def restrictFunctorCongr {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f] [IsOpenImmersion g] :
    restrictFunctor f ≅ restrictFunctor g :=
  SheafOfModules.pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents fun _ ↦ eqToIso (by simp [hf])) ≪≫
    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext : 3; subst hf; simp)

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorCongr_hom_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]
    [IsOpenImmersion g] (M : Y.Modules) :
    ((restrictFunctorCongr hf).hom.app M).app U = M.presheaf.map (eqToHom (by simp [hf])).op := rfl

@[simp]
lemma restrictFunctorCongr_inv_app_app {f g : X ⟶ Y} (hf : f = g) [IsOpenImmersion f]

- **Decompose**: break into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure when available) and solve each individually.
- **Hard bar is a minimum, not a ceiling.** If your objectives specify a "hard bar" (e.g. "add def + pin signature"), that tells you the minimum required — not where to stop. After meeting it:
  - If a recipe exists in `analogies/`, the blueprint chapter has a concrete proof sketch, or you can reason about an approach: attempt the proof body immediately.
  - If no recipe exists: formulate a strategy, attempt it, and leave a partial tactic block at the stuck point. A failed attempt with a `by ... sorry` is far more useful than a clean `sorry`.
  - A bare `sorry` with no attempt is only acceptable when the mathematical obstacle is explicitly named and at least one concrete approach has been tried and documented.
- **After closing all assigned sorries, keep going.** Scan your file for other open `sorry`s that share the mathematical context you've just developed. If you can see a direct path to any of them using lemmas or infrastructure you've already built, attempt them. Stopping at the assigned task boundary when adjacent work is tractable is artificial throttling — you have write permission over your whole file.
- **Comments are not progress. Code is.** If you can describe an approach precisely enough to write it as a comment or TODO, you can attempt it. Any `-- TODO: try X`, `-- could use Y here`, or `/- Next step: ... -/` you write is a sign you stopped too early — attempt X/Y first; only leave the comment if the attempt fails with a named error. The same applies to approaches proposed in task results: if you wrote it in your log as "a possible route", you should have tried it.
- **Autonomous decision-making within your file.** You are fully authorized to make every tactical and mathematical decision within your assigned `.lean` file without waiting for the planner. This includes: choosing proof strategies, adding helper lemmas, attempting adjacent sorries, selecting alternative approaches, deciding proof structure. **Never write "I'll let the planner decide", "I'll defer to the next iteration", or "this requires planner input" for any choice that is entirely within your file.** The planner is not on call; deferring wastes the entire context-loading investment of this session.

  The ONLY actions that require stopping rather than acting:
  - Modifying a *signature* of a protected declaration (you may still fill its proof body).
  - Editing another agent's `.lean` file.
  - Removing an existing public declaration that other files import.
  - Changing the type of an existing declaration in a way that would break other files.

  Everything else — including trying a strategy the planner didn't explicitly endorse — is your call to make and attempt now.

## When to stop

Stop only when progress is blocked by a specific, named obstacle in one of these categories:

1. A genuinely missing Mathlib ingredient — named precisely, attempted via informal agent, no workaround found.
2. A definition or signature that requires another prover's output — name the specific declaration and file.
3. A proof that requires fundamental restructuring of a protected or cross-file definition — name it.

"It seems hard", "the proof is complex", "I couldn't find the right lemma", or "the planner should weigh in on this approach" are NOT valid stop reasons. Document the specific obstacle and keep trying. Only stop when you can write: "I tried approaches X, Y, Z; each fails because [specific mathematical reason]; the informal agent suggested [route] which requires [specific missing ingredient]."

**Specifically banned stop phrases:**
- "I'll wait for the planner to decide."
- "This strategy should be confirmed by the next iteration."
- "I'll defer this choice to the planner."
- "The planner may want to reconsider the approach."

If you find yourself writing any of these: delete the sentence, pick the most promising route, and attempt it. The planner will correct the choice next iter if it was wrong — a failed attempt costs nothing compared to an idle session.

## Completion criteria

Your task is complete ONLY when ALL of:

1. Every `sorry` in scope is replaced with a complete proof.
2. Zero axioms introduced.
3. The file compiles cleanly.

## Never weaken the type to dodge the proof

When the substantive type is unattainable this iter, leave `sorry` with the **intended type signature**. Three patterns are banned:

- **Reflexive-iso placeholder** — replacing `X ≅ Y` with `Nonempty (X ≅ X) := ⟨Iso.refl _⟩`.
- **`Classical.choice` around an explicit witness** — dissolving `Type` vs `Prop` friction without actually constructing the witness.
- **Empty-content `proof_wanted`** — discards the declaration post-elaboration, breaking blueprint cross-references.

**Litmus test**: if you `unfold` your declaration, does it expose the named substantive content or does it stop at `Classical.choice` / `Iso.refl _` / nothing? If the latter, ship the typed `sorry` instead.

## When infrastructure is missing

Do NOT report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before filing "Infrastructure missing":

1. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): call `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` with "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 2.
2. **If the missing ingredient is ≤~100 LOC**: write it as a project-local helper — typed signature plus a genuine proof attempt. A partial proof body is better than a documented gap.
3. **Only after steps 1–2 are exhausted**: write the alternative sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md` with what you tried, why it failed, and the precise statement of the missing ingredient for the plan agent to assign in `mathlib-build` mode.

"Infrastructure missing" as a verdict requires: informal agent called (or key unavailable, documented), local helper attempted if in scope. "I don't see how" is not evidence — a failed attempt with `sorry` at the stuck step IS evidence.

When stuck more generally: break into smaller subgoals, search Mathlib more thoroughly, prove missing helpers yourself, try alternative strategies, re-read the blueprint, use Web Search for published proofs.

**Impossibility vs difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.

## Proof style

- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its body.
- Keep edits minimal; don't delete comments or change labels; don't add unrelated declarations.
- Helper lemmas you introduced may be modified if they turn out wrong.
- Add a concise comment above each helper lemma so reuse is easy.
- **List every new declaration you introduce under a `## Needs blueprint entry` heading in your task_result** (name, file, and the facts its proof relies on) — see Logging below. The project keeps a 1-to-1 Lean ↔ blueprint correspondence: the review agent and planner consume this list (and `archon dag-query unmatched`) to give each new declaration a blueprint block. An unreported helper is an invisible, isolated dependency that silently corrupts the frontier — flagging it is mandatory, not optional. (You never write the blueprint yourself; you only name what needs one.)
- **`change` vs `show`** — `change` reshapes the goal up to defeq; `show` is display-level only. Default to `change` when in doubt.

## Mathlib tags in PROGRESS.md

The plan agent tags suggested lemmas:

- `[verified]` — confirmed to exist.
- `[expected]` — guessed by naming convention. Quick `lean_local_search`; pivot if it doesn't exist.
- `[gap]` — verified NOT in Mathlib. Don't waste search time; formalize a workaround.

## LSP MCP tools

The `archon-lean-lsp` server exposes Lean LSP operations as **MCP tool calls** (`mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_goal`, `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages`, etc.). The lean4 skill reference uses short names (`lean_goal`, …) — same tools, never shell binaries.

- Always invoke through your tool-call interface.
- **Never** call `Bash` with `lean_goal …` — there is no such shell command.
- First LSP action: `mcp__archon-lean-lsp__lean_diagnostic_messages` on your file. If `success: false`, retry once or run `lake build` once via Bash, then retry.

## Search protocol

1. `lean_local_search` first.
2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — describe the mathematical content, not just the name.
3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only.
4. Never use shell `find` / `grep` to locate Mathlib theorems.

## Tooling traps

- **Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics**: in `lean_multi_attempt`, `diagnostics: []` can be misleading. Check that `goals_after` is empty or has advanced.
- **Beware `lean_run_code` with imports**: standalone snippets can silently swallow elaboration errors. Rely on `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.

## Logging

Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` (mirror your `.lean` path: `Algebra/WLocal.lean` → `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`):

```markdown
# Algebra/WLocal.lean

## wLocal_iff (line 45)
### Attempt 1
- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance
- **Dead end:** direct case split without IsLocalRing

### Attempt 2
- **Approach:** Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
- **Result:** RESOLVED
- **Key insight:** `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective` bridges the gap

## Needs blueprint entry
- `Algebra.WLocal.helper_bijective` (line 78) — new helper, no blueprint block yet. Uses: `PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective`. Reviewer/planner: add a `\label` + `\lean` + `\uses` entry so the 1-to-1 correspondence holds.
```

One section per theorem/lemma. Each attempt: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / PARTIAL / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps. Log negative search results. **Always include the `## Needs blueprint entry` section when you added any new declaration** (omit it only if you added none) — it is how the loop keeps Lean ↔ blueprint at 1-to-1.

## End-of-session handoff

**Before declaring done, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote down as a comment, TODO, or "possible route"? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there other open `sorry`s in my file I could attempt with the tools and lemmas I just developed?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before filing "Infrastructure missing"?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped because "it would take too long" or "the planner should decide"? If it's in my file's scope, attempt it.

Only proceed to write the task result when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable."

Before writing results:

1. Write `task_results/<your_file>.md` with current result, lemmas discovered, concrete next step, dead-end warnings.
2. Save all changes; ensure the file compiles.
3. **Write a `## Summary` section** stating: sorry count before → after; exact names of sorries closed; exact names of sorries still open and why; whether you attempted adjacent sorries beyond the assigned ones.
4. **Write a `## Why I stopped` section** — be brutally honest; the planner reads this. **Only Lean code changes count as progress** — a sorry closed, a helper lemma that compiles, a partial tactic block at a specific stuck point. Comments, docstrings, TODO notes, and task result prose do NOT count. If you wrote approaches in comments that you didn't attempt, say so explicitly:
   - `Real progress`: closed N sorries — name each one. State the sorry count before and after.
   - `Partial progress`: made measurable code progress (decomposed into sub-lemmas with proof bodies, closed one branch of a case split) but did not fully close. Name the specific advance and the specific remaining blocker. Decomposing a `sorry` into N named `sorry`s with only comments in their bodies is NOT partial progress unless the bodies contain genuine proof attempts.
   - `Cosmetics only`: changed formatting/comments/style with no proof progress — say so explicitly. **Renaming a `sorry` to a named helper lemma, adding a docstring, or extracting it to a separate declaration with a `sorry` body is `Cosmetics only` unless the body contains a genuine proof attempt.**
   - `Approaches written but not attempted`: you identified routes (in comments, TODOs, or log prose) but did not attempt them — name each one and why you stopped before trying. This is a valid but weak verdict; the planner will re-assign with a directive to attempt them.
   - `Avoided the goal`: attempted something adjacent but not the assigned target — explain why and what you tried instead.
   - `Infrastructure missing`: a specific Mathlib gap was the blocker — name it precisely, confirm the informal agent was called (or key unavailable), describe what it suggested, why it failed, and state the exact missing ingredient statement.
   - `Directive not followed`: explain which part of the planner's directive you deviated from and why.

The dispatch semaphore caps concurrent subagent processes at `loop.max_parallel`; the wrapper resolves `--parent-slug` from `ARCHON_SUBAGENT_SLUG` so nested children inherit the hierarchy.

44	**A dispatch is a BLOCKING Bash call — wait by staying inside it.** `archon-subagent.py` is synchronous (it `subprocess.run`s the `archon subagent` CLI and returns only once the child finishes and its report is on disk). A long dispatch may be auto-backgrounded with a task ID — that's fine; wait for that task's result before acting. **Do NOT use the `Monitor` tool to wait** — it is non-blocking (returns immediately, "keep working"), so your turn ends and the subagents are abandoned mid-run. **Do NOT use foreground `sleep` / `until … ; do sleep … ; done`** — the harness blocks `sleep`. 138	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your file for file-specific hints.
139	- Do NOT edit blueprint chapters. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker; the review agent applies them.
140	
141	### Subagents
142	
143	Subagents are descriptor-driven and discovered at runtime. The catalog is auto-injected into the plan and review prompts each phase (see "Subagents" above for the wrapper invocation). Each subagent reads only what its directive points at — never `PROGRESS.md` / `STRATEGY.md` / phase-agent state — and writes a self-contained report at `task_results/<name>-<slug>.md`. Descriptors with `can_spawn: true` may dispatch children (subject to the global `loop.max_parallel` cap).
144	
145	### Review Agent
146	
147	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for full instructions.
148	- Read `proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data.
149	- Write the session journal to `proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (`summary.md`, `milestones.jsonl`, `recommendations.md`).
150	- Update `PROJECT_STATUS.md` (Knowledge Base only — session narrative goes to `iter/iter-NNN/review.md`).
151	- Maintain the semantic blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE:` annotations, stale `\notready` removal. Normally leave `\leanok` to the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase; only override a marker the sync got demonstrably wrong, and justify it in your summary.
152	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify `PROGRESS.md`.
153	
154	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
155	
156	These run automatically between phases:
157	
158	- **Iter sidecar init** — creates `.archon/iter/iter-NNN/` at iter start so plan + review have a stable destination for per-iter narrative. Top-level files (STRATEGY.md, PROJECT_STATUS.md, task_*.md) stay bounded.
159	- **`sync_leanok`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status.
160	- **`blueprint-doctor`** — runs between prover and review, deterministically detecting orphan chapters, broken `\ref` / `\uses` / `\proves`, and new `axiom` declarations. Findings are written to `logs/iter-NNN/blueprint-doctor.{md,json}` and inlined into the next plan agent's prompt.

50	
51	Blueprint: §Sub-brick A decomposition, `Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`,
52	lemmas `lem:cech_backbone_left_sigma` through `lem:cechSection_contractible`.
53	-/
54	
55	universe u
56	
57	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
58	
59	/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — abstract wide-fibre-power ↔ slice-product
60	
61	These `CategoryTheory`-namespace declarations are the abstract categorical core of the Stub-1
62	geometric backbone decomposition (`cechBackbone_left_sigma`).  They are stated for an arbitrary
63	category (and, downstream, an arbitrary `FinitaryPreExtensive` category) and instantiated at
64	`Scheme` only at the assembly site, so they are reusable and Mathlib-aligned.
65	-/
66	
67	namespace CategoryTheory
68	
69	/-- The wide pullback over `S` of a family `(g k : Z k ⟶ S)`, viewed in `Over S`, is a limit fan of
70	the legs `Over.mk (g k)`: i.e. the wide fibre power over `S` is the product of the legs in the slice
71	`Over S`.  Project-local: Mathlib has `WidePullbackCone.isLimitOfFan` (wide pullback over a terminal
72	base = product of legs) but not this direct slice-product identification of the fibre power. -/
73	noncomputable def widePullback_overX_isLimit {C : Type*} [Category C] {S : C}
74	    {ι : Type*} {Z : ι → C} (g : (k : ι) → Z k ⟶ S) [HasWidePullback S Z g] :
75	    IsLimit (Fan.mk (C := Over S) (f := fun k => Over.mk (g k))
76	      (Over.mk (WidePullback.base g))
77	      (fun k => Over.homMk (WidePullback.π g k) (WidePullback.π_arrow g k))) :=
78	  mkFanLimit _
79	    (fun s => Over.homMk
80	      (WidePullback.lift s.pt.hom (fun k => (s.proj k).left) (fun k => Over.w (s.proj k)))
81	      (WidePullback.lift_base _ _ _ _))
82	    (fun s k => by
83	      apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
84	      change (WidePullback.lift _ _ _ ≫ WidePullback.π g k) = (s.proj k).left
85	      exact WidePullback.lift_π _ _ _ _ _)
86	    (fun s m hm => by
87	      apply Over.OverMorphism.ext
88	      change m.left = WidePullback.lift _ _ _
89	      apply WidePullback.hom_ext
90	      · intro k
91	        rw [WidePullback.lift_π]
92	        exact congrArg CommaMorphism.left (hm k)
93	      · rw [WidePullback.lift_base]
94	        exact Over.w m)
95	
96	/-- The wide fibre power over `S` is the iterated product in the slice: in `Over S` the object
97	`Over.mk (WidePullback.base g)` carrying the wide pullback over `S` is the product
98	`∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (g k)` of the legs.  Project-local foundational step of the Stub-1 backbone
99	decomposition (blueprint `lem:widePullback_overX_eq_prod`). -/
100	noncomputable def widePullback_overX_eq_prod {C : Type*} [Category C] {S : C}
101	    {ι : Type*} {Z : ι → C} (g : (k : ι) → Z k ⟶ S) [HasWidePullback S Z g]
102	    [HasProduct (fun k => Over.mk (g k))] :
103	    Over.mk (WidePullback.base g) ≅ ∏ᶜ fun k => Over.mk (g k) :=
104	  (widePullback_overX_isLimit g).conePointUniqueUpToIso (productIsProduct _)
105	
106	/-- The cofan exhibiting `Over.mk (Sigma.desc f)` as the coproduct of the legs `Over.mk (f i)` in
107	`Over S`.  Abstract version of `AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverCofan`. -/
108	noncomputable def overSigmaDescCofan {C : Type*} [Category C] {S : C} {ι : Type*}
109	    {Z : ι → C} (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) [HasCoproduct Z] :
110	    Cofan (fun i => Over.mk (f i)) :=
111	  Cofan.mk (Over.mk (Limits.Sigma.desc f))
112	    (fun i => Over.homMk (Limits.Sigma.ι Z i) (by simp [Limits.Sigma.ι_desc]))
113	
114	/-- `overSigmaDescCofan` is a colimit: in `Over S` the object `Over.mk (Sigma.desc f)` is the
115	coproduct of the legs `Over.mk (f i)`.  Abstract version of
116	`AlgebraicGeometry.coverArrowOverIsColimit`. -/
117	noncomputable def overSigmaDescIsColimit {C : Type*} [Category C] {S : C} {ι : Type*}
118	    {Z : ι → C} (f : (i : ι) → Z i ⟶ S) [HasCoproduct Z] :
119	    IsColimit (overSigmaDescCofan f) := by
120	  haveI : HasCoproduct (fun i => (Over.mk (f i)).left) := (inferInstanceAs (HasCoproduct Z))

1370	noncomputable def pushPull_eval_prod_iso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]

1372	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj
1373	          (pushPullObj F

1377	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj

1411	
1412	/-- Applying an additive functor `Φ` degreewise to a cochain complex commutes with
1413	augmenting: `Φ(C.augment f) ≅ (Φ C).augment (Φ f)`, with identity components.  This peels
1414	the augmentation node off the evaluated complex `D` so the remaining identification is between
1415	the *non-augmented* complexes. -/
1416	noncomputable def mapHC_augment_iso {V₁ V₂ : Type*} [Category V₁] [Category V₂]
1417	    [Preadditive V₁] [Preadditive V₂] (Φ : V₁ ⥤ V₂) (hΦ : Φ.Additive)
1418	    (C : CochainComplex V₁ ℕ) {Y : V₁} (f : Y ⟶ C.X 0) (w : f ≫ C.d 0 1 = 0) :
1419	    (Φ.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj (C.augment f w) ≅
1420	      CochainComplex.augment ((Φ.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj C) (Φ.map f)
1421	        (by
1422	          haveI := hΦ
1423	          change Φ.map f ≫ Φ.map (C.d 0 1) = 0
1424	          rw [← Functor.map_comp, w, Φ.map_zero]) := by
1425	  haveI := hΦ
1426	  refine HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents
1427	    (fun i => match i with | 0 => Iso.refl _ | _ + 1 => Iso.refl _) ?_
1428	  intro i j hij
1429	  obtain rfl : i + 1 = j := hij
1430	  match i with
1431	  | 0 => simp [CochainComplex.augment]
1432	  | n + 1 => simp [CochainComplex.augment]
1433

1435	degreewise, given the original condition `f ≫ C.d 0 1 = 0`. -/
1436	lemma map_augment_cond {V₁ V₂ : Type*} [Category V₁] [Category V₂]
1437	    [Preadditive V₁] [Preadditive V₂] (Φ : V₁ ⥤ V₂) (hΦ : Φ.Additive)
1438	    (C : CochainComplex V₁ ℕ) {Y : V₁} (f : Y ⟶ C.X 0) (w : f ≫ C.d 0 1 = 0) :
1439	    Φ.map f ≫ ((Φ.mapHomologicalComplex (ComplexShape.up ℕ)).obj C).d 0 1 = 0 := by
1440	  haveI := hΦ

1442	  rw [← Functor.map_comp, w, Φ.map_zero]

1445	base complexes, an isomorphism `eY` of the augmentation objects, and the compatibility square

1447	noncomputable def augmentCochainIso {V : Type*} [Category V] [Preadditive V]
1448	    {C C' : CochainComplex V ℕ} (φ : C ≅ C') {Y Y' : V} (eY : Y ≅ Y')
1449	    (f : Y ⟶ C.X 0) (w : f ≫ C.d 0 1 = 0) (f' : Y' ⟶ C'.X 0) (w' : f' ≫ C'.d 0 1 = 0)

1451	    CochainComplex.augment C f w ≅ CochainComplex.augment C' f' w' := by
1452	  refine HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents
1453	    (fun i => match i with | 0 => eY | n + 1 => HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoApp φ n) ?_
1454	  intro i j hij
1455	  obtain rfl : i + 1 = j := hij
1456	  match i with
1457	  | 0 => exact hcompat.symm
1458	  | n + 1 =>
1459	    simp only [CochainComplex.augment_d_succ_succ, HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoApp]

1467	
1468	/-- **Open-meet identity** (`lem:coverInterOpen_inf_distrib`): intersecting the cover-meet

1470	nonempty `Fin (p+1)`-indexed meet. -/
1471	lemma coverInterOpen_inf_eq_iInf_inf (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {p : ℕ}
1472	    (σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
1473	    coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V = ⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V) := by
1474	  rw [coverInterOpen]
1475	  exact iInf_inf
1476	
1477	/-- **Degreewise object iso of the non-augmented core comparison** (`lem:coreIso_obj_iso`).
1478	The degree-`p` term of the evaluated non-augmented backbone complex
1479	`Γ(V, Ψ(pushPullObj F Y_p))` is isomorphic, as an abelian group, to the degree-`p` term
1480	`∏_σ Γ(⨅ₖ(coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V), F)` of the concrete restricted section Čech complex.
1481	This is `pushPull_eval_prod_iso` (Stub 4) post-composed with the factorwise reindexing along
1482	`coverInterOpen_inf_eq_iInf_inf`. -/
1483	noncomputable def coreIso_objIso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
1484	    (F : X.Modules) (p : ℕ) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
1485	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj
1486	          (pushPullObj F
1487	            ((coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p))))).presheaf.obj
1488	        (Opposite.op V) ≅
1489	      (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).X p :=
1490	  pushPull_eval_prod_iso 𝒰 F p V ≪≫
1491	    Limits.Pi.mapIso (fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
1492	      eqToIso (congrArg
1493	        (fun W => ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W))
1494	        (coverInterOpen_inf_eq_iInf_inf 𝒰 σ V)))
1495	
1496	/-! ### The `coreIso_comm` chain (`lem:coreIso_comm_leg` → `lem:coreIso_comm_coface` →
1497	`lem:coreIso_comm_sum` → `lem:coreIso_comm`), built bottom-up per the iter-072 effort-break. -/
1498	
1499	/-- Application of a finite sum of `Ab`-morphisms distributes over the sum.  (Local copy of
1500	the `CechAcyclic` private helper `ab_hom_finsetSum_apply`.) -/
1501	private lemma abHom_finsetSum_apply {A B : Ab.{u}} {κ : Type*}
1502	    (s : Finset κ) (f : κ → (A ⟶ B)) (t : ToType A) :
1503	    ConcreteCategory.hom (∑ i ∈ s, f i) t = ∑ i ∈ s, ConcreteCategory.hom (f i) t := by
1504	  classical
1505	  induction s using Finset.induction with
1506	  | empty => simp
1507	  | insert a s ha ih =>
1508	      rw [Finset.sum_insert ha, Finset.sum_insert ha, AddCommGrpCat.hom_add_apply, ih]
1509	
1510	set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
1511	/-- **Per-leg naturality of the core comparison coface** (`lem:coreIso_comm_leg`).
1512	For a fixed coface index `k` and multi-index `σ'`, the `σ'`-coordinate (the projection
1513	`Pi.π … σ'`) of the evaluated push–pull coface `G_V(Ψ(δ^nerve_k))` followed by the
1514	degree-`(p+1)` object iso equals the presheaf face restriction `sectionCechFaceRestr σ' k`
1515	applied to the `(σ' ∘ d_k)`-coordinate of the degree-`p` object iso.  This is the genuine
1516	geometric unwinding of `coreIso_objIso` through `pushPull_eval_prod_iso`,
1517	`pushPull_sigma_iso`, the product-leg projection, and `pushPull_leg_sections`. -/
1518	lemma coreIso_comm_leg (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (F : X.Modules)

1794	    non-augmented concrete section Čech complex (with `Fp = (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F`);
1795	  - `ε : Fp.presheaf.obj (op V) ⟶ (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).X 0` is the augmentation map
1796	    (the restriction `t ↦ (t|_{U'_i})_i`); and
1797	  - `hε : ε ≫ (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).d 0 1 = 0`.
1798	
1799	Route (promote degreewise isos to a complex iso):
1800	
1801	(A) DEGREEWISE OBJECT ISO: `pushPull_eval_prod_iso` (Stub 4) gives, for each `p`,
1802	    `D.X (p+1) ≅ (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).X p` as `Ab` objects — both equal `∏_σ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`;
1803	    and `D.X 0 = Fp.presheaf.obj (op V)` matches the augmentation object.
1804	
1805	(B) DIFFERENTIAL MATCH: The differential of `D'` is, read through `sectionCechProductEquiv`
1806	    (`CechAcyclic.lean:1438`), the alternating sum `∑_i (-1)^i • sectionCechFaceRestr(σ,i)`
1807	    (`sectionCech_objD_apply`, `CechAcyclic.lean:1513`). The differential of `D` is the
1808	    evaluation-at-`V` of the Čech-nerve coface maps; under the degreewise identification
1809	    (A), each coface of `D` becomes the corresponding face restriction of `D'`. REUSE
1810	    `sectionCech_objD_apply` rather than rebuilding the alternating-sum bookkeeping.
1811	
1812	(C) ASSEMBLE: Build the `HomologicalComplex.mkIso` (or `HomologicalComplex.Hom` iso) from
1813	    the degreewise components, checking the `comm` condition via the differential match.
1814	
1815	AMBIGUITY FLAG: The type of `Kp` in the definition of `D` uses
1816	`PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)` as a technical adapter between
1817	`SheafOfModules.forget` landing in `PresheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf.val` and the
1818	`PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj` that the evaluation uses. The prover
1819	should verify this adapter type carefully; if the exact path differs from the scaffold,
1820	adjust `Kp` accordingly. Checking how `hSec` in `CechAugmentedResolution.lean:185-205`
1821	constructs `Kp` provides the canonical reference.
1822	
1823	Key Lean names:
1824	- `pushPull_eval_prod_iso` (Stub 4)
1825	- `sectionCech_objD_apply` (CechAcyclic.lean:1513)
1826	- `sectionCechProductEquiv` (CechAcyclic.lean:1438)
1827	- `HomologicalComplex.mkIso` or `HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents`
1828	
1829	Difficulty: MEDIUM (assembly + differential bookkeeping via sectionCech_objD_apply). -/
1830	noncomputable def cechSection_complex_iso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
1831	    (F : X.Modules) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
1832	    let α : X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⟶ X.ringCatSheaf.obj := 𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj
1833	    let cc := ComplexShape.up ℕ
1834	    let K := cechAugmentedComplex 𝒰 F
1835	    let Kp := ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1836	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj K
1837	    let GV :=
1838	      PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf X.ringCatSheaf.obj ⋙
1839	      (evaluation (TopologicalSpace.Opens X)ᵒᵖ AddCommGrpCat).obj (Opposite.op V)
1840	    let D := (GV.mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj Kp
1841	    D ≅ (sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V).augment (sectionCechAugV 𝒰 F V)
1842	      (sectionCechAugV_comp_d 𝒰 F V) := by
1843	  intro α cc K Kp GV D
1844	  -- The push–pull functor `Ψ` through which the evaluated complex `D` is built.  We keep it
1845	  -- inline (rather than abstracted by `set`) so the `Ψ.Additive` instance resolves directly.
1846	  haveI hΨadd :
1847	      (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙ PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).Additive :=
1848	    inferInstance
1849	  haveI : GV.Additive := inferInstance
1850	  -- (CORE, residual) Non-augmented degreewise iso + differential match: the evaluated
1851	  -- non-augmented Čech complex `Γ(V, C•)` is the concrete section Čech complex over the
1852	  -- restricted family `U'_σ = coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V`.  Degreewise object iso is
1853	  -- `pushPull_eval_prod_iso` (Stub 4); the differential match is via `sectionCech_objD_apply`.
1854	  -- `coreIso` and `eY` are kept as `let`-bindings (transparent), so the degree-`0` identity
1855	  -- `(isoApp coreIso 0).hom = (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F 0 V).hom` and `eY.hom = 𝟙` hold definitionally,
1856	  -- which is what makes `hcompat` close (the canonical `sectionCechAugV` is exactly the evaluated
1857	  -- Čech augmentation transported across `coreIso_objIso 0`).
1858	  let coreIso : (GV.mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj
1859	        (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1860	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj
1861	            (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F)) ≅ sectionCechComplexV 𝒰 F V :=
1862	    HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents (fun p => coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F p V)
1863	      (coreIso_comm 𝒰 F V)
1864	  -- (adapter) The augmentation node `GV(Ψ F)` is the section group `Γ(V, F)`: definitional,
1865	  -- since `restrictScalars (𝟙 ·)` and `toPresheaf` leave the underlying abelian-group presheaf
1866	  -- unchanged and evaluation extracts the section over `V`.
1867	  let eY : GV.obj ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1868	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).obj F) ≅
1869	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op V) := Iso.refl _
1870	  -- (compat) The evaluated Čech augmentation equals the canonical `sectionCechAugV` read through
1871	  -- `coreIso_objIso 0`; definitional, since `sectionCechAugV` is by construction
1872	  -- `GV(Ψ(cechAugmentation)) ≫ (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F 0 V).hom` and `eY.hom = 𝟙`.
1873	  have hcompat : GV.map ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1874	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).map (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)) ≫
1875	        (HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoApp coreIso 0).hom = eY.hom ≫ sectionCechAugV 𝒰 F V := by
1876	    have happ : (HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoApp coreIso 0).hom = (coreIso_objIso 𝒰 F 0 V).hom :=
1877	      congrArg Iso.hom (HomologicalComplex.Hom.isoOfComponents_app _ _ 0)
1878	    rw [happ, sectionCechAugV]
1879	    exact (Category.id_comp _).symm
1880	  -- Peel the augmentation node off `D` with `mapHC_augment_iso` (twice), then glue the
1881	  -- non-augmented `coreIso` to the augmentation data with `augmentCochainIso`.
1882	  exact (GV.mapHomologicalComplex cc).mapIso
1883	      (mapHC_augment_iso (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1884	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α) hΨadd (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F) (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)
1885	        (cechAugmentation_comp_d 𝒰 F)) ≪≫
1886	    mapHC_augment_iso GV ‹GV.Additive› (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1887	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F))
1888	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1889	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).map (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F))
1890	      (map_augment_cond (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1891	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α) hΨadd (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F) (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)
1892	        (cechAugmentation_comp_d 𝒰 F)) ≪≫
1893	    augmentCochainIso coreIso eY (GV.map ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1894	        PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).map (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)))
1895	      (map_augment_cond GV ‹GV.Additive› (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1896	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F))
1897	        ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1898	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α).map (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F))
1899	        (map_augment_cond (SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf ⋙
1900	          PresheafOfModules.restrictScalars α) hΨadd (cechComplexOnX 𝒰 F) (cechAugmentation 𝒰 F)
1901	          (cechAugmentation_comp_d 𝒰 F))) (sectionCechAugV 𝒰 F V)
1902	      (sectionCechAugV_comp_d 𝒰 F V) hcompat
1903	
1904	/-! ## Stub 6 — Contracting homotopy on the augmented concrete section Čech complex -/
1905	
1906	/-! ### Engine instantiation for Stub 6.
1907	
1908	The dependent-coefficient combinatorial Čech engine (`CombinatorialCech.depHomotopy_spec`,
1909	CechAcyclic.lean) is instantiated with the augmentation node `Γ(V, F)` as level `0` and the
1910	restricted Čech coefficients `Γ(⨅ₖ (U_{σ k} ⊓ V), F)` as levels `≥ 1`.  The coface maps `δ`
1911	are the presheaf face restrictions (level `0 → 1` is the augmentation restriction), and the
1912	prepend maps `c` are genuine restrictions because `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix` forces
1913	`U'_{i_fix·σ} = U'_σ`.  The `hu`/`hsh` compatibilities collapse to "two parallel restriction
1914	chains between the same opens agree". -/
1915	
1916	section Stub6Engine
1917	
1918	variable (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (F : X.Modules) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
1919	
1920	/-- Composite of two presheaf restrictions is the direct restriction (local copy of the
1921	`CechBridge` private helper `restr_trans`). -/
1922	private lemma stubRestrTrans (P : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u})
1923	    {A B C : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (h1 : A ≤ B) (h2 : B ≤ C)
1924	    (x : ToType (P.obj (Opposite.op C))) :
1925	    ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h1).op)
1926	        (ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE h2).op) x)
1927	      = ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map (homOfLE (h1.trans h2)).op) x := by
1928	  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← P.map_comp, ← op_comp]
1929	  rfl
1930	
1931	/-- Two parallel presheaf restrictions agree (poset-hom uniqueness; local copy of the
1932	`CechBridge` private helper `restr_op_unique`). -/
1933	private lemma stubRestrUnique (P : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Ab.{u})
1934	    {A C : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X} (f g : Opposite.op C ⟶ Opposite.op A)
1935	    (x : ToType (P.obj (Opposite.op C))) :
1936	    ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map f) x = ConcreteCategory.hom (P.map g) x := by
1937	  rw [show f = g from Quiver.Hom.unop_inj (Subsingleton.elim _ _)]
1938	
1939	/-- A nonempty restricted Čech intersection is contained in `V`. -/
1940	private lemma stubInterLeV {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) :
1941	    (⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V)) ≤ V :=
1942	  le_trans (iInf_le _ 0) inf_le_right
1943	
1944	/-- Prepending `i_fix` does not shrink the restricted intersection (positive levels). -/
1945	private lemma stubConsLe {m : ℕ} (i_fix : 𝒰.I₀) (hiV : V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix)
1946	    (σ : Fin (m + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) :
1947	    (⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V)) ≤
1948	      ⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 ((Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin (m + 2) → 𝒰.I₀) k) ⊓ V) := by
1949	  refine le_iInf fun k => ?_
1950	  refine Fin.cases ?_ ?_ k
1951	  · rw [Fin.cons_zero]
1952	    exact le_trans (stubInterLeV 𝒰 V σ) (le_inf hiV le_rfl)
1953	  · intro j
1954	    rw [Fin.cons_succ]
1955	    exact iInf_le _ j
1956	
1957	/-- Prepending `i_fix` to the empty tuple yields an intersection containing `V`
1958	(the augmentation node case). -/
1959	private lemma stubConsLeZero (i_fix : 𝒰.I₀) (hiV : V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix)
1960	    (σ : Fin 0 → 𝒰.I₀) :
1961	    V ≤ ⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 ((Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin 1 → 𝒰.I₀) k) ⊓ V) := by
1962	  refine le_iInf fun k => ?_
1963	  rw [Fin.fin_one_eq_zero k, Fin.cons_zero]
1964	  exact le_inf hiV le_rfl
1965	
1966	/-- The open underlying the level-`m` Stub-6 coefficient: level `0` is `V` (the
1967	augmentation node), level `m+1` is the restricted intersection `⨅ₖ (U_{σ k} ⊓ V)`. -/
1968	private def stubOpen : (m : ℕ) → (Fin m → 𝒰.I₀) → TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X
1969	  | 0, _ => V
1970	  | _ + 1, σ => ⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V)
1971	
1972	/-- The coface inclusion of the Stub-6 opens. -/
1973	private lemma stubOpen_le_coface : ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) (j : Fin (m + 1)),
1974	    stubOpen 𝒰 V (m + 1) σ ≤ stubOpen 𝒰 V m (σ ∘ j.succAbove)
1975	  | 0, σ, _ => stubInterLeV 𝒰 V σ
1976	  | _ + 1, σ, j => le_iInf fun l => iInf_le _ (j.succAbove l)
1977	
1978	/-- The prepend inclusion of the Stub-6 opens (prepending `i_fix` does not shrink the
1979	open, because `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix`). -/
1980	private lemma stubOpen_le_prepend (i_fix : 𝒰.I₀) (hiV : V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix) :
1981	    ∀ {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin m → 𝒰.I₀),
1982	      stubOpen 𝒰 V m σ ≤ stubOpen 𝒰 V (m + 1) (Fin.cons i_fix σ)
1983	  | 0, σ => stubConsLeZero 𝒰 V i_fix hiV σ
1984	  | _ + 1, σ => stubConsLe 𝒰 V i_fix hiV σ
1985	
1986	/-- Dependent coefficient family for the Stub-6 engine: the sections of `F` over
1987	`stubOpen m σ`.  Kept as a reducible abbreviation so the `AddCommGroup` instance is the
1988	generic one on `Ab`-objects (no bespoke match-instance). -/
1989	private noncomputable abbrev cechSectionCoeff (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin m → 𝒰.I₀) : Type u :=
1990	  ToType (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
1991	    (Opposite.op (stubOpen 𝒰 V m σ)))
1992	
1993	/-- The engine coface maps: presheaf face restrictions (level `0 → 1` is the augmentation
1994	restriction `Γ(V) → Γ(U'_σ)`). -/
1995	private noncomputable def cechSectionCoface (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin (m + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)
1996	    (j : Fin (m + 1)) :
1997	    cechSectionCoeff 𝒰 F V m (σ ∘ j.succAbove) →+ cechSectionCoeff 𝒰 F V (m + 1) σ :=
1998	  ConcreteCategory.hom (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.map
1999	    (homOfLE (stubOpen_le_coface 𝒰 V σ j)).op)
2000	
2001	/-- The engine prepend maps: genuine restrictions (level `1 → 0` is the restriction
2002	`Γ(U'_{(i_fix)}) → Γ(V)` along `V ≤ U'_{i_fix}`). -/
2003	private noncomputable def cechSectionPrepend (i_fix : 𝒰.I₀)
2004	    (hiV : V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix) (m : ℕ) (σ : Fin m → 𝒰.I₀) :
2005	    cechSectionCoeff 𝒰 F V (m + 1) (Fin.cons i_fix σ) →+ cechSectionCoeff 𝒰 F V m σ :=
2006	  ConcreteCategory.hom (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.map
2007	    (homOfLE (stubOpen_le_prepend 𝒰 V i_fix hiV σ)).op)
2008	
2009	/-- Transport of a Čech coefficient along an equality of index tuples is the canonical
2010	restriction between the (equal) intersection opens. -/
2011	private lemma cechSectionCoeff_transport {m : ℕ} {τ₁ τ₂ : Fin (m + 1) → 𝒰.I₀}
2012	    (h : τ₁ = τ₂)
2013	    (hle : stubOpen 𝒰 V (m + 1) τ₂ ≤ stubOpen 𝒰 V (m + 1) τ₁)
2014	    (y : cechSectionCoeff 𝒰 F V (m + 1) τ₁) :
2015	    (h ▸ y : cechSectionCoeff 𝒰 F V (m + 1) τ₂)
2016	      = ConcreteCategory.hom
2017	          (((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.map (homOfLE hle).op)
2018	          y := by
2019	  subst h
2020	  rw [show homOfLE hle = 𝟙 _ from Subsingleton.elim _ _, op_id,
2021	    CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
2022	  rfl

2024	/-- Unit compatibility `hu` for the Stub-6 engine: deleting the prepended `i_fix` and then

2027	private lemma cechSection_hu (i_fix : 𝒰.I₀) (hiV : V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix)
2028	    {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)
2029	    (y : cechSectionCoeff 𝒰 F V (m + 1)
2030	      ((Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin (m + 2) → 𝒰.I₀) ∘ (0 : Fin (m + 2)).succAbove)) :

2035	    (le_of_eq (by rw [CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_zero_succAbove i_fix σ]))]

2041	    {m : ℕ} (σ : Fin (m + 1) → 𝒰.I₀) (k : Fin (m + 1))
2042	    (y : cechSectionCoeff 𝒰 F V (m + 1)
2043	      ((Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin (m + 2) → 𝒰.I₀) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove)) :

2048	            ((CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_succAbove_succ i_fix σ k) ▸ y)) := by
2049	  have hle : stubOpen 𝒰 V (m + 1) (Fin.cons i_fix (σ ∘ k.succAbove)) ≤
2050	      stubOpen 𝒰 V (m + 1) ((Fin.cons i_fix σ : Fin (m + 2) → 𝒰.I₀) ∘ (k.succ).succAbove) :=
2051	    le_of_eq (by rw [CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_succAbove_succ i_fix σ k])
2052	  rw [cechSectionCoeff_transport 𝒰 F V
2053	    (CombinatorialCech.cons_comp_succAbove_succ i_fix σ k) hle]
2054	  refine Eq.trans (stubRestrTrans _ (stubOpen_le_prepend 𝒰 V i_fix hiV σ)

2057	    ((stubOpen_le_prepend 𝒰 V i_fix hiV (σ ∘ k.succAbove)).trans hle))).op y) ?_

2062	    ((stubOpen_le_prepend 𝒰 V i_fix hiV (σ ∘ k.succAbove)).trans hle) y

2067	/-- **Coordinatewise identification of the canonical augmentation** (the degree-`0`
2068	augmentation seam of `lem:cechSection_contractible`): the `σ`-coordinate of
2069	`sectionCechAugV` is the plain restriction `Γ(V, F) → Γ(U'_σ, F)`.  This is the `p = 0`

2074	lemma sectionCechAugV_π (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (F : X.Modules)
2075	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) (σ : Fin 1 → 𝒰.I₀) :
2076	    sectionCechAugV 𝒰 F V ≫ Pi.π (fun τ : Fin 1 → 𝒰.I₀ =>
2077	        ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
2078	          (Opposite.op (⨅ l, (coverOpen 𝒰 (τ l) ⊓ V)))) σ =
2079	      ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.map
2080	        (homOfLE (stubInterLeV 𝒰 V σ)).op := by

2083	section Stub6Homotopy

2085	variable (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (F : X.Modules) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)
